title: ANTIOXIDANT COMPOSITION OF BY-PRODUCTS FROM THE CIDER INDUSTRY: APPLE POMACE
author: Pando Bedriñana, Rosa
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The optimization of an ultrasound-assisted extraction method revealed that ethanol concentration significantly influenced extraction efficiency, with higher ethanol levels favoring ursolic acid extraction but reducing total phenol yield. The optimal conditions were determined to be 68% ethanol, 5.1 minutes of extraction time, 90% amplitude, and 0.4 g of sample mass. This method was applied to whole apple pomace (WAP), apple peels (AP), and apple flesh (AF).
steps_raw: The optimization of an ultrasound-assisted extraction method revealed that ethanol concentration significantly influenced extraction efficiency, with higher ethanol levels favoring ursolic acid extraction but reducing total phenol yield. The optimal conditions were determined to be 68% ethanol, 5.1 minutes of extraction time, 90% amplitude, and 0.4 g of sample mass. This method was applied to whole apple pomace (WAP), apple peels (AP), and apple flesh (AF).
keywords: CID-4-PROFIT, cider by-products, apple pomace, bioactive compounds, bioeconomy
